clues.name: Pokédex Habitat
clues.brief: Reveals the habitat category: Grassland, Forest, Cave, Mountain, Sea, WatersEdge, Urban, or RoughTerrain.
clues.tooltip: One of eight habitat categories from the in-game Pokédex. Knowing this rules out large swathes of the Pokédex at once.

clues.name: Can Be Caught in the Wild
clues.brief: Yes/No — does this Pokémon appear as a wild encounter in Red, Blue, or Yellow?
clues.tooltip: A 'No' means it must be obtained another way — evolution, trade, gift, Game Corner, or a special event.

clues.name: Found by Walking
clues.brief: Yes/No — can it be found in tall grass or caves by walking?
clues.tooltip: Does not include Surf or fishing encounters.

clues.name: Found by Surfing
clues.brief: Yes/No — can it be encountered on water while using Surf?
clues.tooltip: Only about 15% of all Pokémon appear this way, so a 'Yes' is quite informative.

clues.name: Found by Fishing
clues.brief: Yes/No — can it be caught using the Old, Good, or Super Rod?
clues.tooltip: Only water-type and a few other Pokémon are fishable.

clues.name: Found in Safari Zone
clues.brief: Yes/No — can it be found in the Safari Zone?
clues.tooltip: About 15 Pokémon are Safari Zone exclusives or primaries. A 'Yes' suggests rare or hard-to-catch species.

clues.name: Obtained from NPC/Trade/Gift
clues.brief: Reveals the specific non-wild obtain method: Starter, Gift, NPC Trade, Fossil, Game Corner, or Special Event.
clues.tooltip: A specific method can immediately identify the Pokémon or narrow it to a very small group.

clues.name: Number of Family Members
clues.brief: Reveals the total Pokémon in this evolutionary family (1, 2, or 3).
clues.tooltip: A family of 3 means a full unevolved→middle→final chain. Combined with Evolution Stage, this is very effective.

clues.name: Current Evolution Stage
clues.brief: Reveals whether this Pokémon is Unevolved, Middle, Final, or Single-Stage.
clues.tooltip: Eliminates a large portion of the Pokédex immediately.

clues.name: Can Evolve
clues.brief: Yes/No — can this Pokémon evolve into something further?
clues.tooltip: 'No' means it's a final-stage or single-stage Pokémon. Cheaper than Current Evolution Stage.

clues.name: Evolves from Another Pokémon
clues.brief: Yes/No — is this Pokémon itself an evolution of something else?
clues.tooltip: Roughly a 50/50 split — one of the most reliably informative yes/no clues.

clues.name: Evolution Method
clues.brief: Reveals how this Pokémon's previous form evolves INTO this one: level-up, item, trade, or none.
clues.tooltip: Important: this describes the method that produces THIS Pokémon from its pre-evolved form. Especially useful for branching evolutions like Eevee's line.

clues.name: Reveal One Weakness
clues.brief: Reveals one weakness not yet shown. Cost increases by +1 per use. Up to 6 times.
clues.tooltip: Each use reveals a different weakness (super-effective type). Cost increases by 1 point with each purchase.

clues.name: Reveal One Resistance
clues.brief: Reveals one resistance not yet shown. Cost increases by +1 per use. Up to 9 times.
clues.tooltip: Each use reveals a different resistance. Cost increases by 1 point with each purchase.

clues.name: Has an Immunity
clues.brief: Yes/No — is this Pokémon completely immune to at least one attack type?
clues.tooltip: About 40% of Pokémon have at least one immunity. A 'Yes' suggests Ghost, Ground, Normal, or Flying types.

clues.name: Reveal One Type
clues.brief: Reveals one of this Pokémon's types, chosen randomly from its available types.
clues.tooltip: Randomly picks type 1 or type 2 (if dual-typed). For single-type Pokémon, always reveals that type.

clues.name: Reveal Second Type
clues.brief: Reveals the OTHER type not shown by 'Reveal One Type'. Requires 'Reveal One Type' first.
clues.tooltip: Only available after purchasing 'Reveal One Type'. Shows the remaining type, or confirms the Pokémon is pure single-type.

clues.name: Base Stat Total Range
clues.brief: Reveals which BST bucket: Under 300 / 300–399 / 400–499 / 500+.
clues.tooltip: Only ~15 Pokémon are in the Under 300 group, making that result very narrowing.

clues.name: Highest Base Stat
clues.brief: Reveals the name of this Pokémon's best stat: HP, Atk, Def, Spc, or Spe.
clues.tooltip: Tells you the Pokémon's battle archetype. Note: Gen 1 uses a unified Special stat (Spc).

clues.name: Highest Stat with Value
clues.brief: Reveals the best stat name AND its exact number (e.g. 'Spc 130'). Requires 'Highest Base Stat' first.
clues.tooltip: Only available after purchasing 'Highest Base Stat'. The exact number narrows the field heavily.

clues.name: Lowest Base Stat
clues.brief: Reveals the name of this Pokémon's weakest stat.
clues.tooltip: A Pokémon with HP as its lowest stat is unusual; Atk as lowest suggests a special attacker.

clues.name: Lowest Stat with Value
clues.brief: Reveals the weakest stat name AND its exact number. Requires 'Lowest Base Stat' first.
clues.tooltip: Only available after purchasing 'Lowest Base Stat'. Extreme low values like 5 or 10 are very rare and nearly uniquely identifying.

clues.name: Reveal Full Stat Spread
clues.brief: Reveals all five base stats: HP/Atk/Def/Spc/Spe — a near-unique fingerprint.
clues.tooltip: Almost no two Pokémon share identical stat spreads. The most powerful single clue — use as last resort. Gen 1 uses a unified Special stat.

clues.name: Used by Any NPC Trainer
clues.brief: Yes/No — does any NPC trainer in RBY use this Pokémon? (Includes Gym Leaders and Elite Four/Rival.)
clues.tooltip: Over 85% of Pokémon appear on at least one NPC trainer team — almost always 'Yes'. Includes Gym Leaders and Elite Four/Rival.

clues.name: Used by a Gym Leader
clues.brief: Yes/No — does any Gym Leader use this Pokémon?
clues.tooltip: About 21% of Pokémon (32 out of 151) are used by at least one Gym Leader. Returns Yes or No only.

clues.name: Used by Elite Four / Rival
clues.brief: Yes/No — is this Pokémon used by the Elite Four or the Rival/Champion?
clues.tooltip: About 33% of Pokémon (51 out of 151) are used by the Elite Four or Rival. Returns Yes or No only.

clues.name: Reveal One Example Moveset
clues.brief: Reveals 4 randomly sampled moves from this Pokémon's full learnset. Cost increases by +1 per use.
clues.tooltip: Each purchase independently samples 4 random moves. The combination reflects the Pokémon's type and role.
clues.example: Surf / Ice Beam / Thunder Wave / Recover

clues.name: Reveal One TM/HM Move
clues.brief: Reveals a TM/HM move not yet shown. No cost increase. Repeatable until all TM/HMs are exhausted.
clues.tooltip: Each purchase reveals a different TM or HM this Pokémon can learn. Once all have been revealed, the clue becomes unavailable.

clues.name: Reveal a Competitive Moveset
clues.brief: Reveals a competitive moveset (randomly chosen from unrevealed ones). Up to 4 purchases.
clues.tooltip: Each purchase reveals a different competitive moveset. When all are revealed, the clue becomes unavailable. Near-uniquely identifying.
clues.example: Thunderbolt / Ice Beam / Recover / Thunder Wave

clues.name: First Anime Appearance
clues.brief: Reveals where this Pokémon FIRST appeared in the anime: the season + episode, or movie/short name.
clues.tooltip: Scope is the original anime: TV Seasons 1–5, the first four films and Pikachu shorts. A couple of Pokémon never appear in scope.
clues.example: S1 (Indigo League) · EP001 — Pokémon: I Choose You!

clues.name: Acquisition / Evolution (Anime)
clues.brief: On-screen events: 'Acquired' (caught, gifted, etc.) and/or 'Evolved', with the season each happened.
clues.tooltip: Only ~55 of 151 Pokémon have any on-screen event. The rest read 'No'. Recorded for BOTH sides of an evolution.
clues.example: Acquired S1 (Indigo League); Evolved S1 (Indigo League)

categories.name: Habitat
categories.desc: Where and how this Pokémon can be found in Red, Blue, and Yellow.
categories.tip: Habitat + Evolution Stage early is a cheap and efficient combo that often narrows the field to under 20 Pokémon.

categories.name: Evolution
categories.desc: Information about this Pokémon's evolutionary family and how it evolves.
categories.tip: Family Members + Evolution Stage costs just 7 pts and is extremely effective.

categories.name: Type
categories.desc: Type matchup information — weaknesses, resistances, immunities, and the Pokémon's own typing.
categories.tip: Has an Immunity (2 pts) is a quick filter before committing to the pricier Type reveals.

categories.name: Stats
categories.desc: Base stat information — how powerful this Pokémon is and where its strengths lie. Gen 1 uses a unified Special stat (Spc).
categories.tip: BST Range + Highest Stat is a great 5-pt combo. Save Full Stat Spread (8 pts) as a last resort.

categories.name: NPC Usage
categories.desc: Whether this Pokémon appears on NPC trainer teams in Red, Blue, and Yellow.
categories.tip: Gym Leader (2 pts) is great value — it reveals the name of the leader, which is very narrowing.

categories.name: Movesets
categories.desc: Information about the moves this Pokémon can learn in Gen 1.
categories.tip: Competitive Moveset (10 pts) is the most powerful clue. Save it for endgame.

categories.name: Anime
categories.desc: How this Pokémon appears in the original anime — Seasons 1–5 plus the first four films.
categories.tip: First Anime Appearance is very revealing when it is a movie or short. Acquisition/Evolution narrows hard for the ~55 Pokémon with an on-screen event.

difficulties.name: Easy
difficulties.desc: Generous — every clue category is available and you start with one free clue.

difficulties.name: Normal
difficulties.desc: All categories available. A solid budget, no handouts.

difficulties.name: Medium
difficulties.desc: Habitat and Anime clues are locked out.

difficulties.name: Hard
difficulties.desc: Habitat, Evolution, Trainers and Anime locked. No second-type reveal. Moveset reveals are limited.

difficulties.name: Extreme
difficulties.desc: Same locks as Hard, plus no type reveals at all and no full stat spread. Tightest budget and harshest clue limits.
